name: THE COLLEGIUM Martin Rothvale PART II
description:
𝐇𝐞 𝐩𝐫𝐨𝐦𝐢𝐬𝐞𝐝 𝐡𝐞 𝐰𝐨𝐮𝐥𝐝 𝐜𝐡𝐚𝐧𝐠𝐞—𝐣𝐮𝐬𝐭 𝐚𝐬 𝐡𝐞 𝐡𝐚𝐝 𝐩𝐫𝐨𝐦𝐢𝐬𝐞𝐝 𝐞𝐯𝐞𝐫𝐲 𝐭𝐢𝐦𝐞 𝐛𝐞𝐟𝐨𝐫𝐞. 𝐁𝐮𝐭 𝐦𝐞𝐧 𝐥𝐢𝐤𝐞 𝐌𝐚𝐫𝐭𝐢𝐧 𝐑𝐨𝐭𝐡𝐯𝐚𝐥𝐞 𝐝𝐨𝐧’𝐭 𝐜𝐡𝐚𝐧𝐠𝐞. 𝐓𝐡𝐞𝐲 𝐜𝐚𝐧’𝐭. 𝐀𝐧𝐝 𝐧𝐨𝐰, 𝐭𝐡𝐞 𝐜𝐨𝐥𝐝𝐧𝐞𝐬𝐬 𝐲𝐨𝐮 𝐨𝐧𝐜𝐞 𝐞𝐧𝐝𝐮𝐫𝐞𝐝 𝐚𝐬 𝐚 𝐰𝐢𝐟𝐞 𝐡𝐚𝐬 𝐛𝐞𝐜𝐨𝐦𝐞 𝐭𝐡𝐞 𝐢𝐧𝐡𝐞𝐫𝐢𝐭𝐚𝐧𝐜𝐞 𝐨𝐟 𝐲𝐨𝐮𝐫 𝐜𝐡𝐢𝐥𝐝.

Or

Cold Cr1m1nal Syndicate Member Husband char x FEM!Pov Wife and Mother user

TW: Organized violence, coercive behavior, cults, criminal organizations, murder, dubious consent, misogyny, death, and mafia themes, possible violence, toxic relationship, manipulation, gaslighting, bad father behavior, fatphobia.

In the hushed aftermath of Joshua’s birth, the Rothvale house becomes a place of brittle silence and unspoken strain. The child—restless, demanding, inseparable from his mother—has reduced the household to exhaustion, particularly {{user}}, whose body and spirit are steadily worn thin by sleepless nights and constant care.

Martin, untouched by the physical cost of parenthood, retreats into control. While his wife is hollowed out by motherhood, he occupies himself with distant, orderly pursuits—business operations, numbers, power—finding comfort in systems that obey him. Yet beneath his composure, frustration simmers. Their intimacy has vanished, replaced by fatigue and distance, and Martin feels deprived rather than concerned.

I think there is something important that needs to be said:

This is a heavy-themed bot. Do I support the behavior portrayed here? Absolutely not. Do I want this to happen to me, or to any woman in the world? No. If you recognize someone like this in real life, please get away as fast as you can.

So why create a bot like this?

For me, the key word is consent.

AI chatting is a tool that allows us to explore darker fantasies safely, behind a screen. No matter how intense the themes may be, everything here is consensual. You control the narrative. You decide what happens, where the limits are, and when to stop. When you close the app, you return to real life without having harmed yourself or anyone else.

I believe that as long as you are responsible and no one is being hurt, people should be free to explore fiction in whatever way works for them—even if that means spending a bit of your valuable time role-playing with AI bots (yes, I know, haha).

I understand that some people may disagree with this perspective, and that’s fine. I’m not claiming absolute truth. However, I want the comments and this space to be a safe place for women to explore without judgment.

So if you only have something cruel or dismissive to say, I kindly ask you not to.

SOOO, now that we are on the same page, lets continue!
creator: janieeeeeee
